description: verifyLicense accepts a CLI-signed valid token and returns { valid:true, claims } with correct tier/exp.
verifyLicense rejects an expired token (exp in past) with reason 'expired'.
verifyLicense rejects a tampered payload (signature mismatch) with reason 'bad_signature'.
verifyLicense rejects an unknown/missing kid with reason 'unknown_kid'.
verifyLicense verifies tokens signed under a rotated (second) kid against the multi-key registry.
verifyLicense rejects a malformed (non-JWS) string with reason 'malformed'.
Signing CLI mints a token that verifyLicense accepts (round-trip with the test keypair).
resolveSelfHostTier returns null when no license_state row exists.
Effective tier = license.tier for a valid, unexpired license.
Effective tier = premium during the 30-day trial window.
Effective tier = essentials after the trial window elapses.
Effective tier = essentials when the license is expired.
Effective tier = essentials for edition_choice = 'ce' (no trial, no license).
Self-host resolution supersedes tenants.plan (plan set to premium but no license -> essentials).
REGRESSION: with no license_state row, SaaS resolution is unchanged (NULL -> pro).
REGRESSION: existing Stripe solo->pro / premium trials still resolve when no license_state row.
verifyLicense result is memoized per token string (second call hits cache).
eeRuntimeEnabled() is false at essentials and true at solo/pro/premium (on an EE build).
TIER_RANK orders essentials below solo; resolveTier handles 'essentials'.
submitLicense stores a valid token and getLicenseStatus then reports 'licensed' with the encoded tier/expiry.
submitLicense rejects an invalid/expired token with a reason and leaves license_state unchanged.
startTrial sets trial_started_at when null and is rejected on a second call (one-trial guard).
startTrial is blocked when the install is already licensed.
